title: Photonic lantern structures and devices
abstract: A photonic lantern couples light from several fibers or fiber cores into one or more fibers or fiber cores. Photonic lanterns are often used to combine several lower-power beams into a single higher-power beam. They can also be used to couple light from multi -core fibers into single-mode, multi-mode, or other multi-core fibers. By modulating the phases of the input beams, the light can be switched from output to output â for example, between output cores of a multi-core output fiber. If desired, the beams can also be amplified using an active fiber in or coupled to the photonic lantern. A first photonic lantern couples signal light and pump light into the core and cladding, respectively, of an active multi-mode or multi-core fiber. And the active multi-mode or multi-core fiber couples amplified signal light into output fiber(s) via a second photonic lantern.
claims:
Claims 1. An apparatus comprising: at least one multi-core optical fiber; at least one optical fiber; and a photonic lantern optically connecting the at least one multi-core optical fiber to the at least one optical fiber. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least one multi-core optical fiber comprises a plurality of polarization-maintaining (PM) cores. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least one multi-core optical fiber comprises a first multi-core fiber and the at least one optical fiber comprises a second multi-core optical fiber. 4. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the first multi-core optical fiber comprises N cores and the second multi -core optical fiber comprises M < N cores, where M and N are positive integers. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein each of the M < N cores is a multi -mode core. 6. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the first multi-core optical fiber comprises N cores and the second multi -core optical fiber comprises M > N cores, where M and N are positive integers. 7. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the photonic lantern is a first photonic lantern coupled to the first multi-core fiber and further comprising: a second photonic lantern coupled to the second multi-core fiber; and an intermediate optical fiber connecting the first photonic lantern to the second photonic lantern. 8. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the intermediate optical fiber is doped with a gain medium. 9. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the intermediate optical fiber is a multi-mode optical fiber. 10. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the intermediate optical fiber is a third multi -core optical fiber. 11. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the intermediate optical fiber has a cladding configured to guide a pump beam. 12. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least one optical fiber comprises a large-mode- area, multi-mode optical fiber. 13. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: at least one pump fiber, evanescently coupled to the at least one multi -core fiber, to guide a pump beam amplifying a signal beam propagating in the at least one multi-core fiber, and wherein the at least one optical fiber has a cladding configured to guide the pump beam. 14. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein at least one of the at least one multi -core optical fiber, the at least one optical fiber, or the photonic lantern has a surface configured to scatter light. 15. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein at least one of the at least one multi -core optical fiber, the at least one optical fiber, or the photonic lantern is embedded in a material configured to couple light out of the at least one multi-core optical fiber, the at least one optical fiber, or the photonic lantern. 16. An apparatus comprising: a multi -core input fiber; a multi -core output fiber; at least one photonic lantern, optically connecting the multi-core input fiber to the multi- core output fiber, to couple a first beam from a first input core into the multi-core input fiber to a first output core in the multi-core output fiber; and a detector, in optical communication with the multi-core output fiber, to sense light emitted by the first output core; a controller, operably coupled to the detector, to determine a phase adjustment to the first beam, the phase adjustment causing the photonic lantern to couple the first input beam from the first input core to a second multi -core output fiber; and 

 a phase modulator, in optical communication with the first input core, to impart the phase adjustment to the first input beam. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the multi -core input fiber has N cores and the multi- core output fiber has N cores, where N is a positive integer. 18. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the at least one photonic lantern has a multi -mode region configured to support M>N modes, where Mis a positive integer. 19. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the at least one photonic lantern has at least M>N evanescently coupled cores, where Mis a positive integer. 20. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the at least one photonic lantern comprises a first photonic lantern and a second photonic lantern, and further comprising: an active fiber connecting the first photonic lantern to the second photonic lantern; and at least one input pump fiber, in optical communication with the active multi-mode fiber via the first photonic lantern, to couple a pump beam into an inner cladding of the active fiber. 21. An apparatus comprising: a multi -core optical fiber to guide signal light; a pump optical fiber to guide pump light; a double-clad active optical fiber having an inner cladding to guide the pump light and a core doped with a gain medium to amplify the signal light; and a photonic lantern optically connecting the multi-core optical fiber and the pump optical fiber to the double-clad active optical fiber.
